ttab.assets.DroppedField.description: DroppedField is one metadata field a crop would drop, as reported by InspectCropMetadata.

addable.description: Addable is true when the field can be preserved by adding it to the whitelist; false for structural drops (thumbnails, binary payloads) that are dropped regardless of configuration.

id.description: Id is the raw underlying identifier, always set (e.g. "ifd0/0x9291", "2:0x64", or an XMP "namespace local").

reason.description: Reason is why the field drops ("not in whitelist", "binary value", "embedded thumbnail", ...).

value.description: Value is a readable representation of the field's value, truncated.

id.description: Id of the asset in the namespace's id scheme.

ns.description: Ns is the source namespace of the asset.

version.description: Version of the asset; defaults to the current version ("0").

dropped.description: Dropped lists the fields a crop would drop, most useful first is not guaranteed — clients should present them grouped by kind.

fields.description: Fields are the configured extras (built-in defaults are not listed).

ttab.assets.MetadataField.description: MetadataField is one recognized field kept on cropped renditions beyond the built-in whitelist. Exactly one group of fields applies per kind.

alias.description: Alias references a built-in EXIF/IPTC field by name (e.g. "DateTimeOriginal", "By-line"), an alternative to spelling out kind, ifd, tag, or dataset when adding a field. Aliases are defined in code and cannot be created through the API. Ignored for XMP.

dataset.description: Dataset is the IPTC-IIM record-2 dataset number for kind "iptc".

exif_ifd.description: ExifIfd selects the EXIF sub-IFD for kind "exif": "ifd0" or "exif".

form.description: Form is the XMP RDF shape for kind "xmp": "simple", "alt", "seq", or "bag".

local.description: Local is the XMP property local name for kind "xmp".

namespace.description: Namespace is the XMP property namespace URI for kind "xmp".

prefix.description: Prefix is the preferred XMP serialization prefix for kind "xmp".

tag.description: Tag is the EXIF tag id for kind "exif" (e.g. 40091). UNDEFINED-typed or oversized values are still dropped by the transform regardless.

id.description: Id of the asset in the namespace's id scheme.

ns.description: Ns is the source namespace of the asset.

denied.description: Denied adds the deny entry when true and removes it when false.

aud.description: Aud is the audience the token is minted for.

exp.description: Exp is the unix expiry; 0 mints a non-expiring token, signed with the public key and only valid at the edge for public renditions.

ext.description: Ext is the format extension, e.g. "jpg"; empty for "original".

id.description: Id of the asset in the namespace's id scheme.

selector.description: Selector defaults to "full"; a soft crop is "c-{x}-{y}-{w}-{h}", optionally carrying a focus point "c-{x}-{y}-{w}-{h}-{fx}-{fy}".

variant.description: Variant names the rendition the token authorizes, including any class suffix (e.g. "preview-wm"), or "original". The signature covers the full path, so the variant is part of what is signed.

version.description: Version of the asset; defaults to the current version ("0").

canonical.description: Canonical is the newline-joined string the MAC covers, for debugging signature mismatches.

kid.description: Kid is the key that signed the token.

path.description: Path is the signed request path, "/v1/{ns}/{id}/{version}/{selector}/{variant}.{ext}".

token.description: Token is the value for the "s" query parameter.

url.description: Url is the ready-to-use asset URL: the signed path plus "?s={token}". Absolute when the service is configured with its external base URL (CDN_BASE_URL); otherwise a path to which the caller prepends the CDN host.

kid.description: Kid is the key identifier carried in minted tokens.

not_after.description: NotAfter is the end of the validity window, unix seconds.

not_before.description: NotBefore is the start of the validity window, unix seconds.

secret.description: Secret is the hex-encoded HMAC key material.

use.description: Use is "delivery" for routinely rotated delivery keys or "public" for the long-lived key that signs permanent public URLs.

id.description: Id of the asset in the namespace's id scheme.

ns.description: Ns is the source namespace of the asset.

pin_selector.description: PinSelector pins the asset to this selector string at the edge (e.g. "c-0.2-0.2-0.5-0.5"). Optional.

version.description: Version narrows the purge to a single version. Optional; the default purges every version.

deleted_objects.description: DeletedObjects is the number of derivatives removed from the bucket.

invalidation_id.description: InvalidationId of the CDN invalidation, empty when the service runs without a CDN distribution configured.

classes.description: Classes are the class names available as a {variant}-{class} suffix, e.g. "wm" makes {variant}-wm a public watermarked rendition.

fit.description: Fit controls how an image is fitted: "inside" scales down into the max bounding box (never upscaling); "cover" crops to the width/height aspect around the selector's focus point and scales to that size.

kind.description: Kind is the asset kind the variant applies to, e.g. "image".

max.description: Max is the bounding box in pixels (longest side) for fit:inside image variants.

name.description: Name of the variant, used as the variant path segment in asset URLs.

public.description: Public marks the variant as a public derivative: reachable with non-expiring (exp=0) tokens.

width.description: Width and Height are the fixed output size of fit:cover variants.

id.description: Id of the asset in the namespace's id scheme.

ns.description: Ns is the source namespace of the asset.

version.description: Version of the asset; defaults to the current version ("0").

state.description: State is "queued" (population is pending, including when it already was), "cached" (an intermediate already exists), or "disabled" (the namespace doesn't use the intermediate cache — not an error, so fire-and-forget callers stay quiet across config changes).

post.description: GetSigningKeys returns the current signing key set: active, upcoming (pre-distributed before their not_before), and recently expired keys. Signers cache the set and select the delivery key whose validity window covers the current time.

GetVariants returns the configured rendition variants. The asset service owns variant geometry, so URL-minting services fetch it from here — caching and refreshing in the background like the signing keys — to learn which variants exist and to compute rendition size hints from an asset's source dimensions.

SignURL mints a signed asset URL server-side from the request parameters, as a debugging aid — the service picks the appropriate key (the public key for a non-expiring exp=0 token, the active delivery key otherwise) and returns the token, path, and canonical string. Production signers mint URLs themselves from GetSigningKeys; this is for inspecting and reproducing signatures. Requires asset_keys_read.

AddMetadataField adds (or updates) one metadata field to preserve on cropped renditions, taking effect without a redeploy. It only widens the recognized-field set; the structural safety filters (no thumbnails, no binary payloads) are enforced in code regardless. Requires asset_admin.

InspectCropMetadata fetches an asset and reports the metadata fields a crop would drop under the current whitelist — known EXIF/IPTC fields with their alias, everything else with its raw id, each with a readable value. A diagnostic for "why did field X disappear from a cropped image". Requires asset_admin.
ListMetadataFields returns the operator-configured metadata fields that extend the built-in cropped-rendition whitelist (spec §7.5).
RemoveMetadataField removes a configured metadata field. Built-in defaults cannot be removed. Requires asset_admin.
RemoveSelectorPin removes a selector pin. Safe once every token minted before the pin was set has expired (the maximum token lifetime is 30 days) and issuance-time enforcement is in place.
SetAudienceDeny adds or removes an audience deny-list entry at the edge: coarse revocation of every URL signed for the audience.

Takedown purges an asset's derivatives from the derivatives bucket and invalidates its CDN paths. Optionally pins the asset to a single selector at the edge (the retroactive-crop kill switch): with a pin set, requests for any other selector are rejected regardless of token validity.

WarmIntermediate queues population of the cached intermediate for an asset, so the first derivative request doesn't pay the original fetch. Fire-and-forget: the response reports queueing, not completion. Requires the asset_warm or asset_admin scope.

tags.description: Keys serves the asset CDN signing keys and delivery configuration to URL-minting services. The asset service is the key authority: it generates and rotates the keys, and both signers and the CDN edge get key material from it, so they can never disagree. Callers authorize with normal client credentials — a scope in their access token grants these methods, nothing more.
tags.name: Keys

tags.description: Management exposes the take-down operations: one authorized call instead of a runbook of raw AWS actions, callable by editorial tooling or event handlers as well as by humans. Note that in the ingest model a take-down only stops new deliveries — customers already hold ingested copies, so the take-down workflow is primarily a customer-notification process with this RPC as one step.
tags.name: Management
